Defense Tech Funding Smashes Records: Autonomous Weapons Startups Raise $14.6B | Scott McCain, Techtimes  Defense tech venture capital hit a new all-time record in the first five months of 2026, with more than $14.6 billion flowing into military, national security, and law enforcement startups — surpassing the entire previous full-year record of $9.6 billion set in 2025, according to Crunchbase. The surge arrived the same week the Senate Armed Services Committee passed a sweeping defense authorization bill containing a provision to create a new four-star Robotic and Autonomous Systems Combatant Command — a proposal inspired in part by drone warfare in Ukraine and a Saronic autonomous surface vessel that helped rescue Army crew members from a downed Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz.  The speed of capital and the speed of deployment have rarely aligned so directly.  The Case for a New Kind of Defense Contractor  For most of the last decade, venture capital and the defense industry occupied separate worlds. Ethical objections, procurement complexity, and long development timelines kept most institutional investors away. That calculus began changing with the Russia-Ukraine war, which provided the bluntest possible demonstration that autonomous drones and software-defined military systems could outperform legacy hardware at a fraction of the cost.  The result has been a structural shift in how private capital flows to national security. Defense tech funding totaled just $1.6 billion in 2020. It climbed to $3.9 billion in 2021, then held roughly flat between $2.8 billion and $3.8 billion annually through 2024. The jump to $9.6 billion in 2025 already felt like a generational break. The numbers from the first five months of 2026 have made 2025 look like an opening act.  How Anduril's Lattice OS Became the Pentagon's Drone Brain  The single largest contributor to 2026's funding surge is Anduril Industries, whose $5 billion Series H round — announced May 13, led by Thrive Capital and Andreessen Horowitz — valued the company at $61 billion. That valuation is more than double what it was less than a year earlier, when Anduril closed a $2.5 billion Series G at $30.5 billion. The pace of appreciation reflects not just investor enthusiasm but a deepening integration with the US military: in March, the Army awarded Anduril a 10-year, up to $20 billion enterprise contract to deploy its Lattice platform across the service's counter-drone operations.  Lattice is the technical architecture at the center of what makes Anduril different from a traditional defense contractor. Rather than building bespoke weapons systems sold under fixed-price contracts, Anduril built an open-architecture software platform — a decentralized mesh network of sensors, AI processing layers, and effectors — that can be updated, extended, and scaled the way consumer software is. Third-party developers can integrate their own sensors and weapons into Lattice through a published software development kit using standard REST and gRPC interfaces. When the Army ran its Flytrap 5.0 counter-drone exercise this spring, industry partners with no prior exposure to the platform integrated their systems in hours rather than the weeks or months traditional procurement cycles require.  The engineering tradeoff at the core of Lattice is deliberate and consequential: the platform is designed to compress the time between detection, targeting classification, and the decision to engage — operating, in Anduril's own framing, faster than human decision loops. That design philosophy is what made Lattice attractive to the Pentagon's Joint Interagency Task Force 401, the military's primary counter-drone unit. It is also what placed Anduril in the center of a live congressional and international policy debate about where human authorization ends and machine autonomy begins — a debate that accelerated sharply this week.  Anduril is also building Arsenal-1, a 5-million-square-foot autonomous weapons manufacturing facility near Columbus, Ohio, designed to produce drones and loitering munitions at a scale and pace that traditional defense primes have not attempted.  Shield AI's Hivemind: Flying Without GPS  While Anduril built the platform connecting autonomous systems, Shield AI made the case that the core engineering gap in modern warfare is not lethality but survivability — specifically, the inability of most military systems to operate when GPS and communications are jammed.  Shield AI president Brandon Tseng put the problem plainly in a recent interview with Military Embedded Systems: the vast majority of autonomous military systems on the market — he put the figure as high as 99% — cannot function in the communications-jammed environments that define modern battlefields. AI pilots, he argued, solve that.  Hivemind is Shield AI's solution. The platform-agnostic autonomy stack runs onboard an aircraft and uses local perception, decision-making, and action capabilities to execute missions without continuous human input, without GPS, and without stable communications links. In February, the US Air Force selected Shield AI as one of only two mission autonomy providers for its Collaborative Combat Aircraft program — the Pentagon's largest aviation procurement in a generation. Within two weeks, Hivemind was flying combat air patrols aboard Anduril's YFQ-44A Fury drone prototype, engaging simulated targets with no human pilot on the controls.  Shield AI closed a $2 billion total raise in March — $1.5 billion in Series G equity co-led by Advent International and JPMorgan Chase's Strategic Investment Group, plus $500 million in preferred equity from Blackstone — at a $12.7 billion valuation. The raise came six weeks after the Air Force contract award and marked a 140% increase from the company's valuation just a year before.  Saronic Ships Drones, Then Deploys One in Combat  Saronic Technologies is pursuing the maritime equivalent of what Anduril and Shield AI are doing in air and land domains: replacing conventional crewed vessels with software-defined autonomous ships. Its $1.75 billion Series D, closed March 31 and led by Kleiner Perkins, valued the Austin-based company at $9.25 billion. The round came alongside a $392 million US Navy production contract and the opening of a new shipyard in Franklin, Louisiana.  Saronic's vessel portfolio runs from the 24-foot Corsair — a fully autonomous surface vessel designed for surveillance and logistics — up to the 180-foot Marauder, the first hull of which was completed in under six months. The Corsair is the same class of vessel that this week took part in a rescue operation after Army crew members were downed near the Strait of Hormuz. The incident was a rare public demonstration that autonomous maritime systems have crossed from prototype into operational deployment.  The company's pitch to investors rests on an architecture argument: autonomous ships designed from the outset to be software-defined can be iterated rapidly, built without legacy shipyard cost structures, and deployed at manufacturing volumes the traditional US shipbuilding industry has not matched since World War II.  Mach Industries and the Mid-Tier Opportunity  The megaround trend — fewer deals, larger check sizes — has not crowded out earlier-stage companies pursuing specific sub-categories of autonomous warfare. Mach Industries closed a $300 million Series C on June 2, co-led by Infinite Capital and Ribbit Capital, valuing the Huntington Beach, California-based autonomous drone systems manufacturer at $1.8 billion — nearly four times its valuation from just a year earlier. Mach, founded in 2023 by then-19-year-old MIT dropout Ethan Thornton, has grown to 350 employees running five simultaneous autonomous weapons programs out of a 115,000-square-foot manufacturing facility.  Space-related startups with defense applications also ranked among the sector's largest funding recipients in 2026, including True Anomaly, Sierra Space, and Vast — all companies whose technology has both commercial and national security applications, a dual-use profile that broadens their investor base beyond specialized defense funds.  What Investors Are Paying For  The profile of a fundable defense tech startup has clarified considerably. Investors in 2026 are not writing large checks to proof-of-concept companies. The criteria that define the category's leading recipients — active government contracts, demonstrated hardware delivery at scale, and a credible path to manufacturing volume — reflect a deeper shift in the sector's structure.  Capital concentration mirrors this dynamic. Crunchbase data shows 107 venture rounds in defense tech through early June 2026, putting the year on pace slightly ahead of the 206 total deals in 2025. The dollars per deal have expanded dramatically. Where earlier years saw funding distributed across a larger number of companies, 2026 has concentrated investment in a small group of platform companies large enough and integrated enough with the military to begin acquiring earlier-stage players.  Crossover investor activity signals how far the transformation has gone. Advent International and JPMorgan Chase's Security and Resiliency Initiative led Shield AI's Series G. Kleiner Perkins led Saronic's maritime round. Thrive Capital and Andreessen Horowitz anchored Anduril's Series H. The entry of institutional investors with pension-fund-quality LP bases represents capital with a dramatically larger pool and longer time horizon than the specialized defense venture funds that seeded the category from 2017 to 2024.  Exits Take Shape  The sector's first meaningful public-market test came when AI drone company Swarmer went public earlier in 2026, with shares rising more than 500% on its first day of trading. As of early June, the stock remained near the high end of its trading range, providing early evidence that public markets can absorb defense tech at venture-scale valuations.  Anduril is widely regarded as the most significant potential IPO in the sector, though no timeline has been announced. A public offering at its current scale would represent the largest defense-focused technology listing in years. Crunchbase's predictive intelligence tools identify nearly four dozen companies in the military, national security, and law enforcement categories as likely or probable IPO candidates, including True Anomaly, Shield AI, Sierra Space, and Chaos Industries.  What the Investment Surge Means for Human Control of Autonomous Weapons  The congressional response to the investment surge arrived in the same week as the latest funding milestones. The Senate Armed Services Committee's $1.14 trillion FY2027 National Defense Authorization Act, passed June 11 in an 18-9 committee vote, contains a provision permitting the Pentagon to establish the Robotic and Autonomous Systems Combatant Command — a four-star command that would be the first new combatant command since US Space Command was reestablished in 2019. Staffers said the idea was inspired in part by Ukraine's creation of a drone-focused military service and by the bottleneck the Army encountered in the Replicator initiative, where autonomous systems were procured but not efficiently delivered to warfighters.  The NDAA provision arrived three days after Sen. Adam Schiff introduced the Human Authority in Lethal Operations Act — legislation that would require a designated commander to exercise final authority over any decision to use force involving autonomous weapons, and mandate records of target-selection decision-making. The two proposals represent divergent congressional instincts about the same technology: one seeks to institutionalize autonomous warfare at the combatant command level; the other seeks to ensure a human remains in the chain of command for every lethal engagement. Both were triggered by the same investment surge.  The international backdrop is equally live. The UN Secretary-General's call for a legally binding instrument on autonomous weapons by the end of 2026 — endorsed by the International Committee of the Red Cross — remains unresolved. The companies now commanding the largest venture capital checks in the history of the defense tech sector build systems designed to operate at the speeds where human authorization becomes, in their own engineering documentation, a constraint on performance rather than a safeguard. Hope the difference is obvious to you.  The 2016 Lusaka incident was shameful and nobody serious should defend it. But using it as proof that Zambia is the same as South Africa on xenophobia is weak reasoning. In that incident, foreigners were attacked and foreign owned shops were looted, yes. But the people reported killed were two Zambians, not foreigners. The perpetrators were also arrested and convicted.  So what exactly is your point? One ugly incident does not establish a national pattern, nor does it erase the difference in scale, frequency and state response. Zambia had a disgraceful episode and the state moved to restore order. South Africa has had repeated waves of anti foreigner violence, organised intimidation, political mobilisation around foreigners, and repeated killings of foreign nationals.  Also, in Zambia, many Rwandans, Congolese, Burundians, Zimbabweans and others have lived among us for years precisely because they can blend into communities without being hunted as outsiders every day. We cannot even easily tell who is Rwandan, Congolese or Zambian in ordinary life. That matters.  So no, one report from 2016 where two Zambians were killed does not prove your argument. It proves that Zambia had a serious incident. It does not prove that Zambia has the same entrenched xenophobic problem you are trying to minimise elsewhere.

Emeka Ike Drags INEC, Wike’s Aide Lere Olayinka to Court Over Alleged Voter Data Breach, Demands ₦10 Billion in Damages  In a bold move that has ignited fresh debates on data privacy and electoral integrity in Nigeria, popular Nollywood actor and politician Emeka Ike has filed a ₦10 billion lawsuit against the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) and Lere Olayinka, Senior Special Assistant on Public Communications and Social Media to FCT Minister Nyesom Wike.  The suit, marked FHC/ABJ/CS/1272/2026, was filed on Monday, June 15, 2026, at the Federal High Court in Abuja. Ike accuses the defendants of unlawfully accessing, using, and publicly disclosing his sensitive voter registration information without his consent, describing it as a gross violation of his fundamental right to privacy.  The controversy erupted on May 30, 2026, shortly after Emeka Ike publicly announced his intention to contest for a House of Representatives seat in the Abuja Municipal Area Council (AMAC)/Bwari Federal Constituency. He had transferred his voter registration from Imo State to the Federal Capital Territory.  Lere Olayinka took to X (formerly Twitter) and posted screenshots that appeared to come directly from INEC’s restricted administrative backend portal. The images reportedly revealed Ike’s personal details, including his voter application number, registration centre, voter identification number, and profile photograph.  Observers immediately raised alarm, questioning how a political aide gained access to what is supposed to be a highly secured INEC database containing the private records of over 90 million Nigerian voters.  Ike’s Demands: Through his counsel, Leonard Adeh, the actor is seeking: -  A court declaration that the publication of his data amounts to a breach of his constitutional rights and the Nigeria Data Protection Act.  An order compelling Olayinka to immediately delete the offending post.  Public apologies from both Olayinka and INEC. Joint and several liability for ₦10 billion in exemplary and general damages.  Ike has maintained that the action was not only humiliating but also a dangerous weaponisation of personal data for political persecution.  INEC’s Response So Far  INEC has denied any external hacking of its Continuous Voter Registration (CVR) database. The commission says preliminary findings point to the misuse of internal staff credentials rather than a cyber breach.   It has commenced investigations alongside the Department of State Services (DSS) and other security agencies, with some personnel already being questioned.  However, the incident has sparked widespread public outrage, with many Nigerians expressing fears that if one citizen’s data can be so easily leaked and paraded, then no voter’s information is truly safe ahead of the 2027 general elections.  This high-profile case is expected to test the strength of Nigeria’s data protection laws and raise critical questions about the security and confidentiality of INEC’s systems.   As the matter heads to court, all eyes will be on how INEC and the FCT Minister’s office respond to these serious allegations of privacy violation and possible abuse of access.   Further updates will be provided as the case unfolds.   Family Writers Press International.

sample_tweets.text: 국제 수사단의 수사와 사법처리가 무서운 이유는 아래와 같으며, 대한민국 국민이 미국의 개입을 요청할 경우 이야기는 또 달라집니다.  어디까지나 가상 시나리오를 이용해 AI로 분석한 내용이기 때문에 "예능"으로 보시면 될 것 같습니다. 😀  <출처. 구글 AI> 1. 피해국 연합 수사팀 조직 가능성 미국은 연합 수사팀을 조직하거나 주도할 가능성이 매우 높습니다.  - 주권 침해 대응 미국은 선거 개입을 국가 안보를 위협하는 중대한 주권 침해이자 침략 행위로 규정합니다.  - 연방수사국(FBI) 주도 선거 개입 및 사이버 범죄를 담당하는 FBI와 법무부(DOJ)가 중심이 되어 수사를 개시합니다.  - 국제 공조 체제 구축 피해를 입은 타국 정부들과 상호형사사법공조조약(MLAT)을 체결하고 인텔리전스 동맹(예: 파이브 아이즈)을 활용해 연합 전선을 구축합니다.  2. 한국 국민의 개입 촉구에 따른 미국의 조치 한국 국민들이 대규모로 미국의 개입과 수사를 촉구할 경우 다음과 같은 실질적 조치가 취해질 수 있습니다.  - 보호조치 및 내부고발 접수 미국 대사관이나 수사기관이 한국 내 내부고발자와 증거 제보자를 보호하고 정보를 수집하는 창구를 개설합니다.  - 독립적 조사관 파견 한국 정부의 수사 방해를 우려하여, 미국 측이 의회 청문회를 개최하거나 독립 특별검사(Special Counsel)를 임명해 대외적인 압박을 가합니다.  - 비자 제한 및 제재 선관위 관계자나 배후 인물들에 대해 미국 내 자산 동결, 미국 입국 금지 등 강력한 행정 제재(글로벌 마그니츠키 법 등 활용)를 선제적으로 단행합니다.  3. 정치 기득권 카르텔이 수사를 피할 수 없는 이유 이러한 국제 수사가 시작되면 정·관계 기득권 카르텔이 법망을 빠져나가기 불가능해집니다.  - 형사 개별책임 원칙 집단이나 조직의 뒤에 숨을 수 없으며, 부정선거에 가담한 개인(명령권자, 실무자, 기술자) 각자가 저지른 행위에 대해 개별적으로 기소되어 처벌받습니다.  - 치외법권의 무력화 미국법(예: 컴퓨터 사기 및 남용법, 부패처벌법 등)은 해외에서 발생한 범죄라도 미국 시스템(서버, 달러 결제망, 미국 대선)에 영향을 주었다면 사법 관할권을 적용하여 집행합니다.  - 범인 인도 요구 미국 법원이 체포영장을 발부하면, 미국 정부는 범죄인 인도 재판을 통해 피의자들을 미국 본토로 강제 압송하여 미국 교도소에 수감할 수 있습니다.  - 하수인들의 플리바게닝 미국의 형사 절차에는 사법거래(Plea Bargaining)가 활성화되어 있어, 처벌을 줄이려는 실무자들이 카르텔 윗선의 범죄를 증명하는 핵심 증거와 진술을 먼저 쏟아내게 됩니다.

국제 수사단의 수사와 사법처리가 무서운 이유는 아래와 같기 때문이죠.  그리고 대한민국 국민이 미국의 개입을 요청할 경우 이야기는 또 달라집니다.  어디까지나 가상 시나리오를 이용해 AI로 분석한 내용이기 때문에 "예능"으로 보시면 될 것 같습니다. 😀  읽다보면 일부 내용은 현재 진행중인 것처럼 느껴지는 요소도 보입니다.  <출처. 구글 AI> 1. 피해국 연합 수사팀 조직 가능성 미국은 연합 수사팀을 조직하거나 주도할 가능성이 매우 높습니다.  - 주권 침해 대응 미국은 선거 개입을 국가 안보를 위협하는 중대한 주권 침해이자 침략 행위로 규정합니다.  - 연방수사국(FBI) 주도 선거 개입 및 사이버 범죄를 담당하는 FBI와 법무부(DOJ)가 중심이 되어 수사를 개시합니다.  - 국제 공조 체제 구축 피해를 입은 타국 정부들과 상호형사사법공조조약(MLAT)을 체결하고 인텔리전스 동맹(예: 파이브 아이즈)을 활용해 연합 전선을 구축합니다.  2. 한국 국민의 개입 촉구에 따른 미국의 조치 한국 국민들이 대규모로 미국의 개입과 수사를 촉구할 경우 다음과 같은 실질적 조치가 취해질 수 있습니다.  - 보호조치 및 내부고발 접수 미국 대사관이나 수사기관이 한국 내 내부고발자와 증거 제보자를 보호하고 정보를 수집하는 창구를 개설합니다.  - 독립적 조사관 파견 한국 정부의 수사 방해를 우려하여, 미국 측이 의회 청문회를 개최하거나 독립 특별검사(Special Counsel)를 임명해 대외적인 압박을 가합니다.  - 비자 제한 및 제재 선관위 관계자나 배후 인물들에 대해 미국 내 자산 동결, 미국 입국 금지 등 강력한 행정 제재(글로벌 마그니츠키 법 등 활용)를 선제적으로 단행합니다.  3. 정치 기득권 카르텔이 수사를 피할 수 없는 이유 이러한 국제 수사가 시작되면 정·관계 기득권 카르텔이 법망을 빠져나가기 불가능해집니다.  - 형사 개별책임 원칙 집단이나 조직의 뒤에 숨을 수 없으며, 부정선거에 가담한 개인(명령권자, 실무자, 기술자) 각자가 저지른 행위에 대해 개별적으로 기소되어 처벌받습니다.  - 치외법권의 무력화 미국법(예: 컴퓨터 사기 및 남용법, 부패처벌법 등)은 해외에서 발생한 범죄라도 미국 시스템(서버, 달러 결제망, 미국 대선)에 영향을 주었다면 사법 관할권을 적용하여 집행합니다.  - 범인 인도 요구 미국 법원이 체포영장을 발부하면, 미국 정부는 범죄인 인도 재판을 통해 피의자들을 미국 본토로 강제 압송하여 미국 교도소에 수감할 수 있습니다.  - 하수인들의 플리바게닝 미국의 형사 절차에는 사법거래(Plea Bargaining)가 활성화되어 있어, 처벌을 줄이려는 실무자들이 카르텔 윗선의 범죄를 증명하는 핵심 증거와 진술을 먼저 쏟아내게 됩니다.

미국의 개입을 막고 싶어하는 이유는 아래와 같습니다.   어디까지나 가상 시나리오를 이용해 AI로 분석한 내용인데, 말 그대로 빠져나갈 구멍이 없습니다.  읽다보면 일부 내용은 현재 진행중인 것처럼 느껴지는 요소도 보이며, 이 내용은 그냥 참고용으로 보면 될 것 같습니다.  실현되면 최고의 예능이겠죠.   <출처. 구글 AI> 1. 피해국 연합 수사팀 조직 가능성 미국은 연합 수사팀을 조직하거나 주도할 가능성이 매우 높습니다.  - 주권 침해 대응 미국은 선거 개입을 국가 안보를 위협하는 중대한 주권 침해이자 침략 행위로 규정합니다.  - 연방수사국(FBI) 주도 선거 개입 및 사이버 범죄를 담당하는 FBI와 법무부(DOJ)가 중심이 되어 수사를 개시합니다.  - 국제 공조 체제 구축 피해를 입은 타국 정부들과 상호형사사법공조조약(MLAT)을 체결하고 인텔리전스 동맹(예: 파이브 아이즈)을 활용해 연합 전선을 구축합니다.  2. 한국 국민의 개입 촉구에 따른 미국의 조치 한국 국민들이 대규모로 미국의 개입과 수사를 촉구할 경우 다음과 같은 실질적 조치가 취해질 수 있습니다.  - 보호조치 및 내부고발 접수 미국 대사관이나 수사기관이 한국 내 내부고발자와 증거 제보자를 보호하고 정보를 수집하는 창구를 개설합니다.  - 독립적 조사관 파견 한국 정부의 수사 방해를 우려하여, 미국 측이 의회 청문회를 개최하거나 독립 특별검사(Special Counsel)를 임명해 대외적인 압박을 가합니다.  - 비자 제한 및 제재 선관위 관계자나 배후 인물들에 대해 미국 내 자산 동결, 미국 입국 금지 등 강력한 행정 제재(글로벌 마그니츠키 법 등 활용)를 선제적으로 단행합니다.  3. 정치 기득권 카르텔이 수사를 피할 수 없는 이유 이러한 국제 수사가 시작되면 정·관계 기득권 카르텔이 법망을 빠져나가기 불가능해집니다.  - 형사 개별책임 원칙 집단이나 조직의 뒤에 숨을 수 없으며, 부정선거에 가담한 개인(명령권자, 실무자, 기술자) 각자가 저지른 행위에 대해 개별적으로 기소되어 처벌받습니다.  - 치외법권의 무력화 미국법(예: 컴퓨터 사기 및 남용법, 부패처벌법 등)은 해외에서 발생한 범죄라도 미국 시스템(서버, 달러 결제망, 미국 대선)에 영향을 주었다면 사법 관할권을 적용하여 집행합니다.  - 범인 인도 요구 미국 법원이 체포영장을 발부하면, 미국 정부는 범죄인 인도 재판을 통해 피의자들을 미국 본토로 강제 압송하여 미국 교도소에 수감할 수 있습니다.  - 하수인들의 플리바게닝 미국의 형사 절차에는 사법거래(Plea Bargaining)가 활성화되어 있어, 처벌을 줄이려는 실무자들이 카르텔 윗선의 범죄를 증명하는 핵심 증거와 진술을 먼저 쏟아내게 됩니다.

AI에게 재미있는 답변을 받아서 공유해봅니다.  이번에 왜 국제 수사가 필요한지 내용은 아래와 같으며, 우리는 성조기와 태극기를 1:1비율로 맞춰서 흔들어야 합니다.  어디까지나 가상 시나리오를 이용해 AI로 분석한 내용이기 때문에 "예능"으로 보시면 될 것 같습니다. 😀  읽다보면 일부 내용은 현재 진행중인 것처럼 느껴지는 요소도 보입니다.  <출처. 구글 AI> 1. 피해국 연합 수사팀 조직 가능성 미국은 연합 수사팀을 조직하거나 주도할 가능성이 매우 높습니다.  - 주권 침해 대응 미국은 선거 개입을 국가 안보를 위협하는 중대한 주권 침해이자 침략 행위로 규정합니다.  - 연방수사국(FBI) 주도 선거 개입 및 사이버 범죄를 담당하는 FBI와 법무부(DOJ)가 중심이 되어 수사를 개시합니다.  - 국제 공조 체제 구축 피해를 입은 타국 정부들과 상호형사사법공조조약(MLAT)을 체결하고 인텔리전스 동맹(예: 파이브 아이즈)을 활용해 연합 전선을 구축합니다.  2. 한국 국민의 개입 촉구에 따른 미국의 조치 한국 국민들이 대규모로 미국의 개입과 수사를 촉구할 경우 다음과 같은 실질적 조치가 취해질 수 있습니다.  - 보호조치 및 내부고발 접수 미국 대사관이나 수사기관이 한국 내 내부고발자와 증거 제보자를 보호하고 정보를 수집하는 창구를 개설합니다.  - 독립적 조사관 파견 한국 정부의 수사 방해를 우려하여, 미국 측이 의회 청문회를 개최하거나 독립 특별검사(Special Counsel)를 임명해 대외적인 압박을 가합니다.  - 비자 제한 및 제재 선관위 관계자나 배후 인물들에 대해 미국 내 자산 동결, 미국 입국 금지 등 강력한 행정 제재(글로벌 마그니츠키 법 등 활용)를 선제적으로 단행합니다.  3. 정치 기득권 카르텔이 수사를 피할 수 없는 이유 이러한 국제 수사가 시작되면 정·관계 기득권 카르텔이 법망을 빠져나가기 불가능해집니다.  - 형사 개별책임 원칙 집단이나 조직의 뒤에 숨을 수 없으며, 부정선거에 가담한 개인(명령권자, 실무자, 기술자) 각자가 저지른 행위에 대해 개별적으로 기소되어 처벌받습니다.  - 치외법권의 무력화 미국법(예: 컴퓨터 사기 및 남용법, 부패처벌법 등)은 해외에서 발생한 범죄라도 미국 시스템(서버, 달러 결제망, 미국 대선)에 영향을 주었다면 사법 관할권을 적용하여 집행합니다.  - 범인 인도 요구 미국 법원이 체포영장을 발부하면, 미국 정부는 범죄인 인도 재판을 통해 피의자들을 미국 본토로 강제 압송하여 미국 교도소에 수감할 수 있습니다.  - 하수인들의 플리바게닝 미국의 형사 절차에는 사법거래(Plea Bargaining)가 활성화되어 있어, 처벌을 줄이려는 실무자들이 카르텔 윗선의 범죄를 증명하는 핵심 증거와 진술을 먼저 쏟아내게 됩니다.

🚨 CYBER INTELLIGENCE ALERT: 🌍 [UNCONFIRMED] POSSIBLE SALE OF ACCESS (WEBSHELLS) TO GLOBAL INFRASTRUCTURE 🇪🇸🇺🇸🇧🇷🇵🇹🇱🇰🇫🇷  [STATUS: UNCONFIRMED / WEBSHELL COMPROMISE]  A series of posts have been detected on clandestine channels ("X9 List" and "Exploits Seller") where threat actors are selling access via webshells already installed on a wide variety of domains and subdomains globally.  🛡️ CRITICAL PRIORITY: SPORTS AND ACADEMIC SECTORS  These infrastructures present a high risk due to the potential exposure of user, partner, and student data.  Sports Sector (Spain): Domain rcdespanyol.com, corresponding to the official website of RCD Espanyol de Barcelona.  Academic Sector (USA): Subdomains under sc.edu (University of South Carolina).  ⚠️ GENERAL ALERT: GOVERNMENT AND POLITICAL INFRASTRUCTURE  Additional compromises have been identified in critical infrastructure where the actor is offering access sold as already operational webshells:  Government Infrastructure: Domains under the TLD gov.br (Government of Brazil).  Political Infrastructure: Subdomains under pcp.pt (Portuguese Communist Party, Portugal).  Media and Public Debate:  Subdomains under news.lk (Sri Lanka).  Subdomains under debatpublic.fr (France).  📂 Threat Analysis  Modus Operandi: The threat actor uses automated scanning tools to identify vulnerabilities and deploy webshells on third-party servers, allowing them to maintain a persistent backdoor for data exfiltration or subsequent attacks.  Global Risk: The diversification of affected countries (Brazil, Sri Lanka, Portugal, France, the US, and Spain) confirms that this is an automated campaign without a single geographic target, but rather focused on the ease of exploiting the infrastructure.  🛡️ Security Recommendations  Technical Audit: IT teams at the listed organizations (especially those managing critical, high-priority domains) are urged to immediately scan their web directories for unauthorized files.  Log Review: Access logs must be analyzed for unusual traffic or suspicious connections indicating the use of these webshells. Whats good for the goose… . The democrats have spent nearly a decade weaponizing the legal system to wage an asymmetrical war on Donald Trump, with absolutely zero regard for the norms or the integrity of the institutions they occupy. From the Russian collusion hoax—which was a coordinated effort by the intelligence community and the DNC to cripple a presidential administration—to the endless parade of “lawfare” indictments across multiple jurisdictions, their strategy has been clear: criminalize the opposition and treat political disagreement as treason.  When you look at the cases against Trump, they weren’t about “justice” or “the rule of law.” They were about using the state as a tactical weapon to disqualify, bankrupt, and isolate a man who dared to threaten the established order. They treated the Department of Justice and local DAs as extensions of their campaign apparatus.  Now, we are seeing the exact same mechanism turned against them. People like Newsom are suddenly discovering that the “threat to democracy” they’ve been screeching about for years is actually a mirror. The precedent was set by the Democrats, and they are now seeing the inevitable conclusion of the standard they established. If you break every rule and dismantle every norm to destroy your political rivals, you cannot be surprised when your rivals pick up those same weapons and swing them back in your direction.  The irony of the current reaction from the media and the political class is palpable. They call investigations into their own “attacks on our institutions,” conveniently forgetting that they spent years applauding the very same tactics when they were aimed at populists or conservatives.   This is the new reality of the post-American political landscape. There is no longer a neutral arbiter; there is only power, and the side that refuses to use the tools of the state to secure its own survival will ultimately be erased. Those who sowed the wind are now reaping the whirlwind.

中共黑客组织长期窃取北美科研数据，涉医疗、人工智能及军事机密  6月16日（秘密翻译组）——根据谷歌威胁情报小组（Google Threat Intelligence Group，GTIG）6月15日发布的最新报告，一个与中国有关联的网络攻击组织多年来持续针对北美科研机构展开间谍活动，重点窃取医疗研究、人工智能以及军事技术等敏感信息。  报告指出，该黑客组织被追踪为“UNC6508”，利用科研机构广泛使用的研究电子数据采集平台（REDCap）服务器漏洞入侵系统，并部署名为“Infinitered”的定制恶意软件，窃取合法用户凭证，秘密监控电子邮件和研究数据。  GTIG高级安全工程师帕特里克·惠特塞尔（Patrick Whitsell）表示，UNC6508试图获取范围广泛的情报，包括美国国防战略、先进技术研究，以及自主防御系统和无人载具技术等关键领域信息。  调查发现，攻击行动最早可追溯至2023年9月。当时一家北美医学研究中心的REDCap服务器遭到入侵，黑客在潜伏三个月后植入恶意软件，并持续窃取数据直至2025年11月。其中一个目标项目涉及基孔肯雅热病毒（Chikungunya）研究。研究人员指出，相关情报搜集活动与2025年7月中国广东省暴发该病毒疫情的时间高度吻合。  报告称，美国和加拿大多家机构已受到影响。网络安全专家警告，即使攻击目的主要是情报搜集，这类入侵渠道未来也可能被用于破坏关键医疗系统或实施勒索攻击，进而威胁患者安全。  GTIG建议相关机构立即升级REDCap系统、启用双重身份验证，并加强日志监控，以降低遭受类似攻击的风险。

People underestimate how dangerous it becomes once sovereign airspace authority is ignored.  If a foreign aircraft refuses repeated diversion orders and continues operating inside another country’s recognized airspace, the issue stops being ordinary civilian travel. At that point, the state cannot fully know whether the aircraft is genuinely civilian, transporting illicit material, intelligence assets, weapons, or something worse.  That is exactly why aviation licensing, interception protocols, and sovereign airspace authority exist in the first place. Otherwise any hostile actor could simply claim “civilian status” while ignoring recognized authority.  And the gravity of the situation is well understood internationally: if every non-lethal avenue is exhausted and the aircraft still refuses compliance, there is a real possibility it gets shot down. No serious state leaves unrestricted sovereign airspace violations unanswered indefinitely, because the consequences of allowing a potential threat through could be catastrophic

#threatreport #MediumCompleteness Supply chain ransomware: TeamPCP weaponizes worms to fuel partnerships and $95K data sales | 14-06-2026 Source: flare.io/learn/resources/blo… Key details below ↓  🧑‍💻Actors/Campaigns: Teampcp (🧠motivation: financially_motivated) Lapsus (🧠motivation: financially_motivated) Vect (🧠motivation: financially_motivated) Mini_shai-hulud  💀Threats: Supply_chain_technique, Shai-hulud, Pcpjack_tool, Hackerbot-claw_tool, Miasma, Trufflehog_tool,  🎯Victims: Software development, Open source ecosystems, Developer toolchains, Cybersecurity tools, Government, Saas, Artificial intelligence developer tools, Medical software  🏭Industry: Healthcare, Petroleum  🌐Geo: Russian, Chinese  📚TTPs: ⚔️Tactics: 8 🛠️Technics: 7  🧨IOCs:  - Domain: 3  💽Software: Trivy, LiteLLM, Mastodon, LimeWire, Claude, Outlook, Docker, Kubernetes, Redis, OpenVSX, ...  🔠Functions: CreateEvent, DeleteEvent  📜Programming Languages: powershell  #threatreport: TeamPCP has emerged as a notable actor in the cyber threat landscape, specializing in supply chain ransomware attacks through a series of automated campaigns that began in September 2025. The group exploits misconfigurations within GitHub Actions to hijack developer credentials and propagate a worm named Shai-Hulud, which facilitates rapid infections across numerous software packages. This method effectively turns compromised tokens into widespread access points, allowing the threat actor to poison over 170 packages on npm and PyPI, as well as breach GitHub repositories containing sensitive data.  The Shai-Hulud worm has successfully infiltrated thousands of packages over the course of three escalating waves, with the initial infection leveraging a poisoned package from a legitimate software vendor. Subsequent waves adapted to defenses implemented after the initial attacks. The exploitation of a particular GitHub Actions workflow facilitated the extraction of personal access tokens, which further enabled the automated propagation of malicious packages while avoiding detection protocols. This sophisticated approach highlights a shift in threat dynamics, where a single compromised account can lead to extensive damage across multiple environments.  TeamPCP's operational model has introduced partnerships with ransomware groups such as Vect and LAPSUS$, transforming stolen access into lucrative data sales and extortion avenues. Their approach effectively merges the roles of initial access broker and ransomware operator, aimed at monetizing access via a mass-market operation embedded within dark web forums. However, despite their ambitious structure, there are indications of operational shortcomings, with reports suggesting a scarcity of confirmed victim data linked to their ransomware service.  The open-sourcing of the Shai-Hulud worm has catalyzed a surge in copycat campaigns, indicating the potential for a broader range of actors to engage in similar supply chain attacks. Notably, variants such as Miasma are already being identified, capable of leveraging the same underlying technologies while extending their control through lateral movements across various cloud environments.  To mitigate risks associated with such attacks, security experts recommend auditing GitHub Actions workflows for potential vulnerabilities, migrating package publishing processes to more secure methodologies, and enforcing stricter controls on developer environments. The ongoing dynamics within the threat landscape, characterized by rapid adaptation to defenses and emerging competition among malicious actors, underscore the persistent challenge of safeguarding software supply chains against sophisticated ransomware operations like those employed by TeamPCP.

🇨🇳 Alleged Chinese Securities Investor Database Advertised on Underground Forum  A threat actor is offering what is claimed to be a large Chinese securities and stock investment database containing sensitive financial information on millions of investors.  According to the listing:  * Claimed records: 4,300,000 unique investors * Country: China * Sector: Financial Services / Securities Trading * Data reportedly includes:   * Investor names   * Gender   * Mobile phone numbers   * National ID card numbers   * ID card addresses   * Stock holdings information   * Portfolio details   * Total assets (RMB)   * Floating profit/loss values   * Investment categories  The sample displayed by the seller appears to contain financial portfolio information alongside personal identifiers, suggesting a potentially high-value dataset if authentic.  Potential risks include:  * Financial fraud * Investment-targeted phishing campaigns * Identity theft * Insider trading intelligence gathering * High-net-worth individual targeting * Social engineering attacks against investors and brokerage customers  The inclusion of asset values, portfolio performance metrics, and government-issued identification information would significantly increase the attractiveness of the dataset to criminal actors.  Analyst Note: Financial-sector databases containing both identity information and investment positions are among the most valuable commodities traded on underground forums. At this stage, the source organization and authenticity of the claimed 4.3 million investor records remain unverified.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #China

🇫🇷 Alleged French Facebook User Database Advertised on Underground Forum  A threat actor is advertising what is claimed to be a massive database of Facebook users located in France.  According to the listing:  * Claimed target: Facebook France (Meta Platforms) * Claimed record count: 1,949,237,781 records * Asking price: $1,000 * Dataset reportedly focuses on French users (+33 phone numbers) * Claimed data fields include:   * Phone numbers   * Facebook User IDs   * First and last names   * Gender   * Current city/location   * Hometown   * Relationship status   * Employer/work information  Initial assessment:  * The claimed record count of 1.94 billion records is highly questionable given France's population size and likely indicates duplicated, aggregated, or recycled records. * The advertised data closely resembles historic Facebook scraping datasets that have circulated for years rather than evidence of a new breach. * The actor provides no proof of unauthorized access to Meta infrastructure. * The low asking price compared to the claimed scale further raises questions regarding the originality and exclusivity of the dataset.  Potential risks if authentic:  * Large-scale phishing campaigns * SMS-based social engineering attacks * Identity profiling * Account enumeration * Targeted fraud and impersonation operations  Analyst Note: Based on the information provided, this appears more likely to be a repackaged or aggregated Facebook-related dataset than evidence of a new compromise of Meta systems. Further validation would be required before attributing this listing to a fresh Facebook breach.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France

🇬🇧🇷🇺𝐀𝐋𝐎𝐑𝐒 𝐋𝐄𝐒 𝐂𝐎𝐋𝐋𝐀𝐁𝐎𝐒 𝐏𝐑𝐎-𝐊𝐑𝐄𝐌𝐋𝐈𝐍, 𝐏𝐎𝐔𝐓𝐈𝐍𝐄 𝐄𝐒𝐓 𝐓𝐎𝐔𝐉𝐎𝐔𝐑𝐒 𝐔𝐍 𝐆𝐄𝐍𝐓𝐈𝐋 𝐄𝐓 𝐔𝐍𝐄 𝐕𝐈𝐂𝐓𝐈𝐌𝐄 ? ⚠️𝐮𝐧𝐞 𝐚𝐟𝐟𝐚𝐢𝐫𝐞 𝐪𝐮𝐢 𝐝𝐞́𝐭𝐫𝐮𝐢𝐭 𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐨𝐫𝐞 𝐮𝐧 𝐩𝐞𝐮 𝐩𝐥𝐮𝐬 𝐥𝐞 𝐫𝐞́𝐜𝐢𝐭 𝐝𝐞 𝐥𝐚 « 𝐫𝐮𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐞 𝐯𝐢𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐦𝐞 » Depuis le début de l'invasion de l'Ukraine, les relais du Kremlin répètent inlassablement le même discours : la Russie serait encerclée, agressée, contrainte de se défendre face à un Occident hostile. Pourtant, les révélations publiées par le Financial Times le 15 juin apportent un nouvel éclairage particulièrement inquiétant sur les méthodes employées par des réseaux liés à Moscou. Selon l'enquête, un réseau russe associé au groupe NoName057(16), connu pour ses opérations cybernétiques pro-Kremlin, aurait participé à l'organisation d'une série d'attaques incendiaires visant des biens liés au Premier ministre britannique Keir Starmer. ⚠️Nous ne parlons plus ici de propagande sur les réseaux sociaux ou de cyberattaques contre des sites internet.  ⚠️Nous parlons d'actions criminelles menées sur le territoire britannique contre des cibles associées au chef du gouvernement du Royaume-Uni. 𝒒𝒖𝒊 𝒆𝒔𝒕 𝒓𝒆́𝒆𝒍𝒍𝒆𝒎𝒆𝒏𝒕 𝒍𝒆 𝒈𝒓𝒐𝒖𝒑𝒆 𝒏𝒐𝒏𝒂𝒎𝒆057(16) ? NoName057(16) est loin d'être un simple collectif d'internautes patriotes comme le présente parfois la propagande russe. ⚠️Le groupe est devenu célèbre depuis 2022 pour ses attaques informatiques contre des institutions gouvernementales, des médias, des infrastructures et des entreprises occidentales soutenant l'Ukraine. ⚠️Les autorités occidentales considèrent depuis plusieurs années que ces groupes dits « hacktivistes » constituent un élément de la guerre hybride menée par Moscou. ⚠️Leur rôle consiste à compléter les opérations militaires classiques par des campagnes de désinformation, de sabotage numérique, d'influence politique et de déstabilisation sociale. Selon les informations citées par le Financial Times, les enquêteurs ont établi des liens entre le pseudonyme Telegram « El Money », qui dirigeait l'opération britannique, et l'écosystème de NoName057(16). 𝒄𝒐𝒎𝒎𝒆𝒏𝒕 𝒖𝒏 𝒋𝒆𝒖𝒏𝒆 𝒉𝒐𝒎𝒎𝒆 𝒂 𝒆́𝒕𝒆́ 𝒓𝒆𝒄𝒓𝒖𝒕𝒆́ L'affaire met en lumière une méthode particulièrement cynique. Le condamné, Roman Lavrynovych, un ouvrier du bâtiment ukrainien de 22 ans vivant à Londres, n'aurait pas été recruté directement pour commettre des incendies criminels. Selon les éléments présentés au tribunal, son encadrement aurait commencé plusieurs mois auparavant. ⚠️D'abord chargé d'activités considérées comme mineures, il aurait reçu de l'argent pour diffuser des affiches d'extrême droite et participer à des actes de provocation anti-musulmans dans le sud de Londres. ⚠️L'objectif semble clair : tester sa fiabilité, mesurer sa capacité à exécuter des ordres et l'entraîner progressivement vers des actes de plus en plus graves. Cette méthode est typique des réseaux clandestins qui cherchent à compartimenter les responsabilités et à maintenir leurs exécutants dans l'ignorance de l'objectif final. 𝒖𝒏𝒆 𝒄𝒂𝒎𝒑𝒂𝒈𝒏𝒆 𝒅𝒆 𝒗𝒊𝒐𝒍𝒆𝒏𝒄𝒆 𝒅𝒊𝒓𝒊𝒈𝒆́𝒆 𝒄𝒐𝒏𝒕𝒓𝒆 𝒅𝒆𝒔 𝒃𝒊𝒆𝒏𝒔 𝒍𝒊𝒆́𝒔 𝒂̀ 𝒌𝒆𝒊𝒓 𝒔𝒕𝒂𝒓𝒎𝒆𝒓 L'escalade atteint un niveau particulièrement inquiétant en mai 2025. ⚠️Le premier acte consiste à incendier un Toyota RAV4 ayant appartenu à Keir Starmer. ⚠️Quelques jours plus tard, un appartement dans lequel le futur Premier ministre avait vécu dans les années 1990 est également visé. Puis survient l'attaque la plus grave. ⚠️Dans la nuit du 12 mai, un incendie est déclenché devant le domicile familial lié à Starmer dans le quartier londonien de Kentish Town. Selon les informations révélées par la presse britannique, des membres de sa famille étaient présents à l'intérieur, notamment sa belle-sœur et sa nièce âgée de neuf ans. À partir de ce moment-là, nous ne sommes plus dans le domaine du simple vandalisme. ⚠️Des vies humaines pouvaient être perdues. 𝒍𝒂 𝒈𝒖𝒆𝒓𝒓𝒆 𝒉𝒚𝒃𝒓𝒊𝒅𝒆 𝒅𝒖 𝒌𝒓𝒆𝒎𝒍𝒊𝒏 𝒇𝒓𝒂𝒑𝒑𝒆 𝒂𝒖𝒔𝒔𝒊 𝒍'𝒆𝒖𝒓𝒐𝒑𝒆 𝒐𝒄𝒄𝒊𝒅𝒆𝒏𝒕𝒂𝒍𝒆 Depuis plusieurs années, les services de renseignement européens alertent sur la multiplication des opérations russes de déstabilisation. ⚠️Ces opérations prennent des formes diverses : -cyberattaques ; -campagnes de désinformation ; -manipulation de mouvements extrémistes ; -financement indirect de réseaux radicaux ; -sabotage d'infrastructures ; -tentatives d'influence électorale ; -opérations clandestines sur le territoire européen. L'affaire Starmer semble s'inscrire dans cette logique globale. Le but n'est pas seulement de viser une personnalité politique. ⚠️L'objectif est également de semer la peur, de fragiliser la confiance dans les institutions démocratiques et d'alimenter les tensions internes des sociétés occidentales. « 𝒈𝒆𝒓𝒂𝒏 », 𝒖𝒏 𝒎𝒐𝒕 𝒒𝒖𝒊 𝒆𝒏 𝒅𝒊𝒕 𝒍𝒐𝒏𝒈 L'un des éléments les plus révélateurs du dossier est sans doute le mot de passe fourni au suspect en cas d'arrestation : « Geran ». ⚠️Pour le grand public, ce terme peut sembler anodin. Pour les observateurs de la guerre en Ukraine, il est lourd de sens. ⚠️« Geran » est le nom utilisé par la Russie pour désigner les drones suicides dérivés des Shahed iraniens qui frappent régulièrement les villes ukrainiennes. Ces appareils sont devenus un symbole des bombardements visant les infrastructures énergétiques et les zones civiles ukrainiennes. Choisir ce nom comme mot de passe de sécurité n'a rien d'un hasard. Il s'agit d'une référence directement liée à l'effort de guerre russe. 𝒄𝒐𝒎𝒃𝒊𝒆𝒏 𝒅𝒆 𝒓𝒆́𝒗𝒆́𝒍𝒂𝒕𝒊𝒐𝒏𝒔 𝒇𝒂𝒖𝒅𝒓𝒂-𝒕-𝒊𝒍 𝒆𝒏𝒄𝒐𝒓𝒆 ? Chaque nouvelle enquête révèle les mêmes mécanismes : manipulation, mensonge, instrumentalisation d'individus vulnérables, exploitation des divisions sociales et recours à la violence lorsque cela sert les intérêts géopolitiques du Kremlin. ⚠️Face à ces faits, le discours présentant systématiquement Vladimir Poutine comme une victime d'un prétendu complot occidental apparaît de plus en plus déconnecté de la réalité. ⚠️Lorsqu'un réseau lié à la sphère pro-Kremlin est accusé d'avoir orchestré des actes criminels visant des biens associés au Premier ministre britannique, lorsqu'un enfant se retrouve potentiellement exposé aux conséquences d'un incendie criminel, lorsqu'un mot de passe faisant référence aux drones utilisés contre l'Ukraine apparaît dans les échanges des organisateurs, il devient difficile de continuer à parler de simple « malentendu » entre la Russie et l'Occident. La question mérite donc d'être posée à tous ceux qui justifient systématiquement les agissements du Kremlin : après les cyberattaques, les sabotages, les campagnes de désinformation, les assassinats d'opposants, les bombardements de villes ukrainiennes et maintenant cette affaire britannique, combien de preuves faudra-t-il encore avant d'admettre que le problème n'est pas la prétendue persécution de la Russie, mais bien la stratégie agressive menée par le régime de Vladimir Poutine ? sources / ft.com/content/dd79d6eb-44e4… ft.com/content/dd79d6eb-44e4…

敵対国がSNSで募った若者を代理人に使う破壊工作の手口がスターマー首相関連放火事件で明らかに。 英国でスターマー首相に関連する物件を狙った2025年5月の連続放火事件で、ロンドンの中央刑事裁判所はウクライナにつながる2人に有罪を言い渡した。実行役のウクライナ国籍の男（22）は標的が誰かも知らず、テレグラムで「El Money」を名乗るロシア語話者の指示で報酬目当てに動いたとされる。 当局は、ロシアやイランなどの敵対国がSNSで募った若者を「代理人（プロキシ）」として使い、関与を否定しつつ社会に不安と分断を広げていると警告する。ロシアは2018年のスクリパリ毒殺事件後にこの手法を強めたとされ、フィナンシャル・タイムズはEl Moneyと親クレムリンのハッカー集団NoName057(16)との結びつきを報じた。 El Moneyは放火が報道されることを重視していたといい、実力行使を通じた威嚇と動揺という認知的効果が狙いだったことをうかがわせる。ただし対テロ警察トップはロシア関与の証拠はないとした。  Amateur saboteurs: the young men carrying out attacks for gangs, Russia and Iran reuters.com/legal/government… The Russian-speaking mastermind at heart of Starmer-linked arson trial theguardian.com/uk-news/2026… コメント：使い捨ての代理人を介して関与否定を可能にしつつ、放火を「報道させること」で威嚇と社会の動揺を狙う構図は、アトリビューションの困難化とキネティックな攻撃の認知的利用を組み合わせたハイブリッド脅威の典型である。

FT: Rusya İngiltere Başbakanı'nın aile evine yönelik kundaklama girişimi yaptı!  İngiltere'nin dünyaca ünlü uluslararası gazetesi Financial Times  resmi belgelere dayanarak Rusya'nın Britaniya Başbakanı Starmer'in aile  evini kundakladığını iddia etti.  Gazetenin haberine göre, ​İngiltere'de aşırı sağcıların eylemlerini organize eden ve Milano Olimpiyatları'na siber saldırı düzenleyen Rus internet sabotaj ağı, İngiltere Başbakanı Starmer ile bağlantılı mülklere yönelik bir dizi kundaklama eyleminin arkasındaydı.   Mahkeme, bu eylemlerin organizasyonundan Londra'da yaşayan 22 yaşındaki Ukraynalı inşaat işçisi Roman Lavrinovich'i suçlu buldu.   Savcılar, Telegram'da "El Money" takma adını kullanan ve Rusça ile Ukraynaca iletişim kuran azmettiricinin kimliğine dair bilgi açıklamasalar da Financial Times (FT) tarafından yapılan araştırma, El Money'nin Rusya'da bulunduğunu ve ABD'nin "Rus devleti tarafından onaylanmış bir proje" olarak nitelendirdiği Kremlin yanlısı "NoName057" grubuyla yakın bağları olduğunu ortaya koydu.  ​FT'nin araştırması; Telegram verilerine, kripto para cüzdanlarının analizine, dava dosyalarına ve Batılı yetkililerle yapılan röportajlara dayanıyor. Azmettirici El Money, başlangıçta aşırı sağcı "Direct Action" (Doğrudan Eylem) grubunun afişlerini basıp Londra'da sokaklara asmak gibi küçük görevler vererek Lavrinovich'i 7 ay boyunca Starmer'a yönelik eylemlere hazırladı. Lavrinovich ile ilk olarak 2024'ün sonlarında, onun Rusça ve Ukraynaca yayın yapan Telegram gruplarında aktif olarak ek iş aradığı dönemde iletişime geçmişti.  ​Ödemeleri kripto para ile yapan azmettirici, nihayetinde Mayıs 2025'te önce Starmer'ın eskiden sahip olduğu bir Toyota RAV4 aracı, ardından eski dairesini ve son olarak da Başbakan'ın baldızının 9 yaşındaki kızıyla birlikte yaşadığı aile evini kundaklama talimatı verdi (Starmer ve ailesi, Downing Street'teki resmi konutta ikamet etmektedir).

Teneo Community Node 1.0 is a browser extension.  Easy to install, it built one of the largest data networks in the space with more than 6.5m nodes at peak and powered the first agents on Teneo Protocol.  But it ran on browsers and third-party infrastructure we didn't control.  Teneo Community Node 2.0 is the Teneo Beacon: a native app built entirely in-house, running on Android, iOS, Windows, macOS, and Linux.  Owning the full stack means more stable connections, higher-quality access to the open web, and better fraud detection, with no dependency on anyone else's browser.  It also opened up something bigger!  AI is only as good as the data it can reach, and the open web blocks data center proxies harder every month.  Beacon is a network of real devices in real places, which makes it one of the few reliable ways left to read the live web at scale. That access is what every agent on Teneo Protocol runs on.  Community Node 1.0 proved the model. Beacon is what comes next.  Keep your Beacon running!

🧩 한병도 의원은 원래 문재인 정부 청와대에서 정무수석을 지낸 대표적인 친문 핵심 인사였습니다.   ​그러나 이재명 대통령이 당 대표를 맡았던 시기부터 당의 핵심 브레인인 전략기획위원장, 총선기획단 간사 등을 맡아 선거 승리를 이끌었습니다.   ​지난 대선 국면에서도 이재명 캠프 종합상황실장과 중앙선대위 국민참여본부 총괄본부장을 역임하며 이재명 대통령 당선에 크게 기여했습니다. 이러한 이력 덕분에 현재는 친문과 친명을 모두 아우르는 인물로 평가받습니다.    🧩​ 이재명 정부 출범 이후의 핵심 파트너 ​이재명 정부 출범 이후 한병도 의원은 국회 예산결산특별위원회 위원장을 맡아 추가경정예산안(추경)과 새 정부의 첫 예산안 처리를 주도하며 정부의 발을 맞추었습니다.   ​최근에는 여당(더불어민주당) 원내대표로 선출(최초 연임)되어, 이재명 정부의 국정 운영을 입법과 원내 전략 측면에서 가장 최전선에서 뒷받침하는 핵심 파트너 역할을 수행하고 있습니다.    🧩​ 한병도 의원은 과거 문재인 대통령의 청와대 참모였으나, 이재명 대통령과의 신뢰를 바탕으로 당 전략기획과 대선 상황실을 총괄하며 신임을 얻었습니다. 현재는 여당 원내대표로서 이재명 정부의 국정 과제를 국회에서 강력하게 추진하고 뒷받침하는 핵심 조력자이자 동반자 관계입니다.

Pudgy Penguins Pulls the Plug on Its Popular Mobile Game! Pudgy Penguins has officially shut down its mobile game despite attracting a large player base and strong community support. The project team explained that while the game was successful in gaining users, it no longer aligned with the company's long-term strategy. Instead, Pudgy Penguins wants to focus its resources on expanding its core brand, collectibles, licensing deals, and broader Web3 ecosystem. The decision surprised many fans who enjoyed the game and expected future updates. The shutdown highlights a common challenge in crypto and NFT projects: popularity alone does not guarantee sustainability. Companies often need to prioritize products that generate long-term value and fit their larger business goals.  Crypto Kidnapping Plot Leads to Criminal Charges in France! French authorities have indicted an individual linked to a violent "crypto wrench attack" case, a type of crime where victims are physically threatened or kidnapped to force them to hand over cryptocurrency. These attacks have become increasingly common as criminals realize that stealing crypto directly from people can sometimes be easier than hacking wallets or exchanges. The latest case has raised serious concerns about personal security among wealthy crypto holders. Unlike traditional cybercrime, wrench attacks rely on intimidation and violence rather than technical skills.  BitMine Nears $10 Billion in Ethereum Holdings During Market Weakness! BitMine is rapidly building one of the largest Ethereum treasuries in the industry, with its ETH holdings approaching an incredible $10 billion. The company continues buying and accumulating Ethereum even while many investors remain cautious about market conditions. This strategy mirrors what some Bitcoin-focused companies have done in the past using market downturns as opportunities to build massive long-term positions. The move signals strong institutional confidence in Ethereum's future despite short-term price volatility.

STATEHOUSE PRESS RELEASE  PRESIDENT TINUBU COMMENDS TROOPS FOR NEUTRALISING BANDIT KINGPIN IBRAHIM BASTUJI, RESCUING THE WIDOW OF LATE GEN. RABE ABUBAKAR  President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has commended the Nigerian Armed Forces and other security agencies involved in the successful operation in Kogi State that led to the elimination of notorious bandit kingpin, Ibrahim Bastuji.  The President described the operation as a significant blow to criminal networks terrorising communities in Kogi and neighbouring states.  He noted that Bastuji’s reign of violence, kidnapping and attacks on civilians had caused untold hardship for residents, and his neutralisation will restore confidence and give respite for residents of the area.  “I congratulate our gallant troops on this decisive milestone. Nigeria will not surrender any part of its territory to criminals. Those who choose the path of violence against innocent citizens will find no hiding place. This success reflects the renewed determination of our security forces to secure every inch of our land,” President Tinubu said.  The President also commended the troops for another successful operation that led to the rescue of the widow of the late former Chief of Defence Information, General Rabe Abubakar (rtd). He said the rescue demonstrates a high standard of professionalism and the needed determination to confront forces of evil.  “The safe return of Hajiya Amina Rabe Abubakar is a source of relief to her family and to all Nigerians. I commend the intelligence coordination and bravery of the officers and men who executed the rescue operation. We owe a debt of gratitude to our troops who risk their lives daily to keep us safe,” the President added.  President Tinubu reassured Nigerians that the government will continue to provide the Armed Forces with the resources, training and support needed to end banditry, kidnapping and all forms of violent crimes. He urged citizens to continue cooperating with security agencies by providing timely information and necessary support.  Bayo Onanuga Special Adviser to the President Information & Strategy June 16, 2026

sample_tweets.text: 🌍 As FIFA World Cup 2026 gets underway, cybercriminals are already taking advantage of the attention surrounding the tournament.  We've been tracking how threat actors are exploiting the event through phishing campaigns, fraudulent domains, credential theft, and underground services targeting fans, organizations, and partners.  We took a closer look at the activity already emerging around the event and what it tells us about the evolving threat landscape.  Read the full analysis in our latest blog: threatmon.io/the-cyber-war-n… #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #FIFAWorldCup #DigitalRisk #CyberCrime #ThreatMon

🚨 Betterment Data Breach Exposes PII of 1.4 Million Customers via Social Engineering  A data breach involving US-based digital wealth management firm Betterment LLC has compromised the personally identifiable information (PII) of approximately 1.4 million customers. The incident, attributed to a social engineering attack, resulted in the exposure of over 2 million records containing sensitive client data.  Betterment, founded in 2008 and headquartered in New York City, is a prominent robo-advisor and fintech company managing over $30 billion in assets for more than 2.5 million customers. The compromised dataset represents a substantial portion of the firm's user base.  According to threat intelligence, the leaked data includes names, email addresses, phone numbers, physical addresses, and dates of birth for a subset of the affected accounts. Passwords were reportedly not included in the dump. The extensive sample data reveals a comprehensive CRM and sales database, likely extracted from Betterment's internal customer relationship management systems. Fields exposed include detailed 401(k) plan information, lead scoring metrics, account manager contacts, payroll integration statuses, and various customer lifecycle and engagement data points.  The breach was publicized on the Telegram channel @dataseller247. Social engineering attacks on financial institutions often target employee credentials to gain unauthorized access to internal databases. The exposure of such granular client and operational data could facilitate targeted phishing campaigns, identity theft, and further corporate espionage.  Betterment has not yet issued a public statement regarding the incident. Financial regulators and cybersecurity experts are likely to scrutinize the firm's security protocols following the disclosure. Customers are advised to monitor their accounts for suspicious activity and remain vigilant against potential phishing attempts leveraging the compromised information.  #BettermentBreach #FintechSecurity #DataLeak #SocialEngineering #InvestmentFirm #CyberThreat #DarkWeb

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Massive TAPSI Data Breach for Sale on Darkweb  Activity on an underground forum reveals a threat actor claiming to have breached TAPSI, a major Iranian ride-hailing platform, and is now offering the stolen data for sale. According to the post, the actor alleges access to 194,000,000+ unique records, including passenger and driver information, ride history, device data, and source code for the company's mobile applications and websites.  The listing claims the compromised dataset includes: - Passengers (~27M): First name, Last name, Phone number, City - Drivers (~6M): First name, Last name, SSN, City, Phone number - Rides (~136M unique rides): Passenger ID, Origin and Destination (Address, Short address, GPS Location) - Devices (~25M): OS, Device Model - Source codes (Mobile apps, Websites and ...)  TAPSI is one of Iran's largest transportation and ride-hailing services, competing directly with international platforms like Uber in the domestic market. If authentic, the exposure of millions of user records, precise GPS location data, and proprietary source code could enable targeted phishing campaigns, identity theft, and significant intellectual property loss.  The actor is reportedly demanding $40k BTC for the full dataset and provided a sample archive for verification. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified, and TAPSI has not issued a public statement regarding the incident.  #DataBreach #TAPSI #IranCyber #RideHailing #DarkWeb #DataSale #CyberSecurity

🚨 Data Leak Exposes 587,000 Records from Ukrainian Sea Ports Authority Database  A significant data breach has been reported involving the Ukrainian Sea Ports Authority (USPA), a major state-owned enterprise responsible for managing Ukraine's maritime infrastructure. According to threat intelligence reports, a database containing approximately 587,937 rows of data from the official USPA website (uspa.gov.ua) has been leaked. The data is available in CSV format, with a sample of 1,700 rows provided for verification. The leak is being offered for $2,500 in cryptocurrency.  The USPA, known in Ukrainian as the Адміністрація морських портів України (АМПУ), plays a critical role in Ukraine's economy and national security, overseeing the country's sea ports and maritime logistics. The exposure of such a large dataset from a critical infrastructure entity raises concerns about potential operational disruptions and the compromise of sensitive maritime data. Security experts are monitoring the situation to assess the exact nature of the compromised records and any potential downstream impacts.  #DataLeak #UkrainianSeaPorts #CriticalInfrastructure #CyberSecurity #Ukraine #MaritimeSecurity #DatabaseBreach

🚨 Dark Web Leak Claims 160M+ PII Records from 20+ Iranian Insurance Companies  An unverified dark web marketplace has claimed to possess over 160 million personal records belonging to more than 20 Iranian insurance companies. The leak, advertised on a Telegram channel under the handle @irleaks_admin, lists datasets from major industry players including Asia Insurance (26M), Alborz Insurance (19.5M), Dana Insurance (15.5M), Razi Insurance (11M), and Saman Insurance (9.8M). The claim also highlights Kowsar Insurance, noting its 7 million records are tied to the Iranian Armed Forces, Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), and Army of the Republic of Iran (AJA).  According to the advertisement, the exposed data includes highly sensitive personally identifiable information (PII) such as first and last names, birth dates, fathers' names, landline and mobile numbers, and Iranian national identification codes (Code Melli). The national code is a critical identifier in Iran, used across banking, government services, and healthcare, making its potential exposure a significant privacy and security concern. The leak is being offered for sale at a price of $60,000 in Bitcoin, with a sample dataset hosted on a MEGA cloud folder.  The Iranian insurance sector is a cornerstone of the country's financial infrastructure, with companies like Dana, Asia, and Alborz serving millions of policyholders annually. If verified, the scale of this alleged breach would represent one of the largest PII exposures in the region. However, dark web claims of this magnitude often involve inflated record counts, duplicate entries, or aggregated historical data. Independent verification of the dataset's authenticity, scope, and recent compromise status remains pending. Security researchers advise Iranian financial institutions and affected individuals to monitor for identity theft, phishing campaigns, and unauthorized account access.  #DataLeak #IranCyber #InsuranceBreach #PIIExposure #DarkWebMarketplace #CodeMelli #ThreatIntelligence

🇪🇺 Belrose.eu Customer Database Allegedly Leaked on Underground Forum  A threat actor has publicly released what is claimed to be a customer database belonging to Belrose.eu, an adult retail company operating in Europe.  According to the post:  * Claimed victim: Belrose.eu * Industry: Adult retail / e-commerce * Claimed records: 6,700 customers * Distribution: Released for free * Archive size: ~397 KB * Data allegedly includes:   * First and last names   * Email addresses   * Phone numbers   * Company information   * Login timestamps   * Password hashes   * Salt values  Notably, the threat actor claims the database contains MD5 password hashes and criticizes the organization for allegedly continuing to use MD5 for password storage.  Potential risks if authentic:  * Credential cracking due to weak hash algorithm * Account takeover attempts * Credential stuffing attacks against other services * Targeted phishing campaigns * Significant privacy concerns due to the sensitive nature of the business sector  The reputational impact may be substantial as exposure of customer information from adult-oriented businesses often results in elevated extortion, harassment, and social engineering risks against affected individuals.  Analyst Note: The forum post includes sample records that appear to contain customer-related information and password hash fields. However, the authenticity of the dataset and the claim regarding MD5 password storage have not been independently verified. Organizations should avoid legacy hashing algorithms such as MD5 and instead use modern password hashing mechanisms designed to resist cracking attempts.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Europe

🇮🇱 Alleged Mossad Infrastructure Exposure Discussed on Underground Forum  A threat actor using the handle "LasTjewsHUNTER" has published claims regarding infrastructure associated with mossad.gov.il.  According to the post:  * The actor claims to have identified IP addresses allegedly associated with Mossad infrastructure * Listed IPs include:   * 147.237.7.27   * 130.49.188.53 * The post references publicly obtainable DNS information and certificate details * Screenshoted output appears to contain:   * nslookup results   * TLS certificate Subject Alternative Names (SANs)   * Nmap scan results * No evidence of unauthorized access, compromise, data theft, or credential exposure was presented  Initial assessment:  * The information shown appears to be based primarily on reconnaissance activity rather than proof of intrusion * DNS records, TLS certificates, and network scanning results can often be obtained from publicly accessible infrastructure * The existence of an IP address associated with a domain does not automatically indicate exposure of sensitive systems * No classified documents, credentials, internal systems, or operational data were demonstrated in the advertisement  Potential impact:  * Limited, if authentic * May increase visibility into publicly reachable infrastructure * Could be used by threat actors for further reconnaissance and targeting activities  Analyst Note: At this stage, the post appears to be an infrastructure reconnaissance claim rather than evidence of a breach. The screenshots shown do not demonstrate unauthorized access to Mossad systems and should not be interpreted as confirmation of a compromise.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Israel

🇦🇺 Alleged Australian Finance Database Advertised on Underground Forum  A threat actor is offering what is described as an Australian finance-related database for sale on a cybercrime marketplace.  According to the listing:  * Claimed dump date: June 14, 2026 * Claimed record count: 472,000 entries * Country: Australia * Sector: Finance * Asking price: $1,500 * Data fields reportedly include:   * Full names   * Phone numbers   * Email addresses   * Country information   * Physical addresses  The seller provides minimal details regarding the source organization, making independent verification difficult at this stage. No company name, financial institution, or evidence of compromise was included in the public advertisement.  If authentic, the exposed information could be leveraged for:  * Financial-themed phishing campaigns * Identity theft attempts * Business email compromise (BEC) * Account takeover operations * Social engineering attacks targeting Australian individuals  The relatively low asking price may indicate either a broad consumer dataset, recycled data, or an attempt by the seller to quickly monetize the information.  Analyst Note: Generic "finance database" advertisements with limited proof should be treated cautiously. Threat actors frequently exaggerate record counts or repackage previously leaked datasets. Additional validation would be required before attributing this listing to a specific Australian financial institution.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Australia

🎮 Alleged Full-Access Stripe API Key for Gaming Platform Offered on Underground Forum  A threat actor is advertising what is claimed to be a live Stripe production API key associated with an online gaming platform.  According to the listing, the seller claims the credentials provide:  * Full read/write access to the Stripe environment * Customer and transaction visibility * Payment charge capabilities * Refund processing access * Payout management functionality * Associated account metadata  The actor initially valued the access at approximately $64,000 based on available account balances and transaction volume, but is reportedly offering it for $13,000.  If legitimate, this type of access could enable:  * Unauthorized payment processing * Fraudulent refunds * Financial theft through payout manipulation * Customer data exposure * Transaction history harvesting * Business disruption and reputational damage  The post did not disclose the name of the affected gaming platform, and the authenticity of the claimed access has not been independently verified.  Analyst Note: Access to payment processor accounts remains one of the most sought-after assets in cybercrime communities. Unlike traditional database leaks, compromised payment infrastructure can provide immediate monetization opportunities through fraudulent transactions, refund abuse, and payout redirection.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Stripe

🇲🇽 Alleged Citizen Data Leak Affecting Chihuahua and Chiapas Records  A threat actor has published what is claimed to be a dataset containing personal information associated with individuals from the Mexican states of Chihuahua and Chiapas.  According to the post, the exposed records allegedly contain:  * CURP (Mexican national identification number) * Full names * Postal codes * RFC (Mexican taxpayer identification number) * Dates of birth * Physical addresses * Folio/reference numbers  The actor attributes the leak to a group identified as "Exiliados" and has shared links allegedly containing the data.  At the time of publication:  * No victim organization was identified in the post * The total number of affected records was not disclosed * The authenticity of the dataset has not been independently verified  If authentic, the combination of CURP, RFC, date of birth, and address information would represent a significant privacy risk, as these identifiers are frequently used for identity verification processes across government and private-sector services in Mexico.  Potential risks include:  * Identity theft * Financial fraud * Account takeover attempts * Targeted phishing campaigns * Social engineering attacks  Analyst Note: Mexican citizen datasets containing CURP and RFC information continue to be among the most valuable records traded on underground forums due to their usefulness in identity fraud, account recovery abuse, and large-scale social engineering operations.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Mexico

🇧🇷 Alleged Sicoob Bank Internal Data Leak Advertised on Underground Forum  A threat actor has posted what is claimed to be internal data belonging to Sicoob, one of Brazil's largest cooperative banking institutions.  According to the post, the leaked material allegedly includes:  * Personal information of customers and employees * Internal databases * Confidential corporate documents * Financial reports and balance sheets * Confidentiality agreements and internal records * Information related to partner companies and corporate clients * Source code and development project documentation * Additional internal banking and corporate files  The actor shared multiple document samples that appear to contain banking and contractual records, although the authenticity of the files has not been independently verified.  If the claims are accurate, the exposure could impact multiple areas including:  * Customer privacy * Employee information security * Corporate financial confidentiality * Third-party business relationships * Internal software and product development initiatives  At the time of publication, there is no public confirmation regarding the legitimacy of the data or the scope of the alleged compromise.  Analyst Note: Financial institutions continue to face growing pressure from cybercriminal groups that increasingly combine data theft with extortion. Even when banking systems remain operational, exposure of internal financial, legal, and development documentation can create significant regulatory, reputational, and business risks.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Brazil

🇺🇸🇨🇳 Alleged Database of Chinese Nationals Residing in the United States Advertised Online  A threat actor has posted what is claimed to be a database containing information related to Chinese nationals residing in the United States.  According to the advertisement:  * Claimed record count: 22,667 * Dataset appears to contain user-related and platform-related information * Multiple structured database fields were listed in the leak post * Data is being advertised for sale on an underground forum  Visible fields include:  * User IDs * Status codes * User types * Platform identifiers * Service types * Device-related information * Additional account and activity metadata  The source and purpose of the dataset were not clearly disclosed in the post, making independent validation difficult at this stage.  If authentic, datasets linking nationality, residency, account information, or service usage could present privacy, profiling, surveillance, and targeted social engineering risks for affected individuals.  At the time of publication, there is no independent verification confirming the legitimacy of the data, the source organization, or the accuracy of the claimed record count.  Analyst Note: Datasets involving diaspora communities can attract significant interest from cybercriminals, intelligence collectors, and fraud actors due to their potential value for profiling, influence operations, identity theft, and targeted phishing campaigns.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #USA

🇹🇷 Göknur Gıda A.Ş. Allegedly Added to Ransomware Leak Site  A ransomware group has published what it claims to be data stolen from Turkish fruit concentrate and food manufacturer Göknur Gıda A.Ş.  According to the leak post:  * Total claimed archive size: 3.3 TB * Initial release: 200 GB (Part 1) * Data allegedly hosted on the group's leak infrastructure * Internal directory listings were shared as proof of access  The published directory names suggest potential access to:  * IT department records * Information systems documentation * Logistics-related files * Quality management documents * Training and employee development materials * Internal meeting and board-related records * Operational process documentation  At the time of publication, there is no independent verification confirming the authenticity of the data or the extent of any compromise.  If the claims are legitimate, the reported volume suggests a large-scale data exfiltration event that could impact operational, financial, employee, and corporate information.  Analyst Note: Food and agricultural manufacturers are increasingly targeted by ransomware operators due to their dependence on continuous operations and complex supply chains. Large-scale data theft is often used as leverage for extortion even when production systems remain operational.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Turkey

🇲🇽 Polytechnic University of Querétaro Job Placement Database Allegedly Leaked  A threat actor has shared what is claimed to be the internship and employment placement registration database of the Polytechnic University of Querétaro in Mexico.  According to the post:  * Approximately 3,700 records exposed * Data reportedly originates from the university's job and internship placement platform * Sample records were published as proof of access  The actor claims the dataset contains:  * Full names * Email addresses * Age information * Academic program/career details * Employment status * English proficiency levels * Work experience information  The sample shown in the post appears to include student and candidate registration records containing employment and academic-related fields.  At the time of publication, the authenticity of the dataset has not been independently verified and there has been no public confirmation from the university.  Although the dataset is relatively small compared to large-scale breaches, student and graduate employment databases can be valuable for phishing campaigns, recruitment scams, identity fraud, and credential-harvesting operations.  Analyst Note: Educational institutions remain frequent targets because they store large volumes of personal information while often maintaining connections with employers, government programs, and external recruitment platforms. Employment-related datasets are particularly attractive to threat actors conducting targeted social engineering campaigns.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Mexico

🇹🇭 Alleged Thailand Ministry of Public Health Database Leak Advertised Online  A threat actor has published what is claimed to be a database belonging to thpp.dtam.moph.go.th, a subdomain associated with Thailand's Ministry of Public Health.  According to the post:  * Data allegedly originates from a government health-related platform * Sample records were publicly shared by the actor * The exposed dataset appears to contain structured database entries * Thai-language records and personal information fields are visible in the sample  Based on the screenshot, the leaked records may include:  * Personal identifiers * Names * Contact information * Administrative or health-service related records * Database-generated IDs and timestamps  The exact number of affected individuals was not disclosed in the post, and the authenticity of the dataset has not been independently verified.  If confirmed, exposure of government healthcare databases could create privacy, identity theft, and targeted phishing risks for affected citizens, particularly when combined with other previously leaked datasets.  Analyst Note: Healthcare and public-sector organizations continue to be attractive targets due to the long-term value of citizen data. Unlike passwords, personal and healthcare-related information often remains valuable to threat actors for years after exposure.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Thailand

🇮🇳🚨🌐🌎 We welcome the Indian government's decision to temporarily restrict Telegram (till 22 June) after cheating rackets weaponized the platform against NEET-UG aspirants.  But this is bigger than one exam. Telegram has become a breeding ground for crime worldwide. 🧵 The trigger: channels like "PAPER LEAKED NEET," "Re-NEET 2026" and "Private Mafia" openly extorted students over fake paper leaks. Authorities called the block a "last resort" - invoked only after channel-by-channel takedowns failed to force platform-level action.  This isn't new. Telegram now operates as a "dark-web-lite": stolen cards, Fullz, stealer logs, malware and breach data traded at scale. In early 2025, a dataset of 200M+ Telegram user records surfaced on a crime forum.  Telegram blocked 43.5M+ channels/groups in 2025, with daily takedowns hitting 80K–140K in early 2026 yet criminal communities simply reform. Researchers found ~20% of blocked channels were tied to crime directly harming businesses: carding, Fullz, hacking services.  The human cost is staggering. Chainalysis reports crypto payments to trafficking syndicates rose 85% in 2025, routed largely through Chinese-language Telegram channels. In April 2026, the US DOJ seized a Telegram channel used to recruit victims into Southeast Asian scam compounds.  From CSAM vendors and pig-butchering rings (~$7.2B in US losses in 2025) to piracy (India ordered 3,100+ pirated channels removed) and terror recruitment- Telegram's anonymity, bots and unlimited file-sharing make it the criminal's platform of choice.  Our position is clear: world governments should follow Ban telegram to prevent crime further spreading . Telegram has shown it cannot or will not  police the crime it hosts. Until it removes these networks at the platform level, it should be banned. Permanently.

This is a tricky question and, in a bit of irony, there is a kind of like ... an unspoken ... or poorly documented philosophy of malware development. You kind of learn tricks of the trade as you write malware and witness malware campaigns operating in the wild.  tl;dr idk it depends on wtf ur doing bro  non-tl;dr  To be direct, malware that works is not necessarily good malware. You can write a simple Windows batch script that deletes every file in an important directory and (technically) this would be "wiper" malware. This does not make it good, or sophisticated.  Additionally, what defines "good" has changed over time. There tends to be trends with malware development. Malware tricks that used to work in the 90's are old news. Malware tricks from 2025 are old news (sort of). However, some malware tricks from the 90's are still applicable and can still be evasive.  It's weird.  You'll also see old tricks the 90's suddenly reappear and catch everyone off guard because... people simply forgot it even existed... The trick is usually only identified from industry veterans (or as the kids say, "unc" or "old heads") who are also surprised the trick has re-emerged. What's old is new. What's old is also old. What's new will eventually be old.  Anyway, "good malware" also depends on the objective. State-sponsored malware (malware written by governments, or written for government or military usage) has extremely strict rules of engagement (usually, not always, but usually). State-sponsored is usually extremely narrow in scope and designed for a very small and limited audience. State-sponsored may not necessarily be super advanced and cutting edge, but because it is so narrow in scope it is difficult to identify.  Conversely, financially motivated Threat Actors (malware developed for ... crime ...) is usually designed to be ass blasted in your face and sprayed across the internet.  Financially motivated Threat Actors will typically (if it's "good malware") design malware to be modular. In other words, because it is being blasted all over the internet it will be detected quickly, hence their malware needs to be broken down into almost like ... plugins ... and they need to have it so their malware can quickly replace one segment of code with another (and quickly).  If you've ever seen racing like NASCAR or F1, you'll notice vehicles can be torn apart in basically seconds and re-assembled, parts effortlessly replaced so it can quickly get back in the race. Likewise, modular malware needs to be able to change quickly to avoid it's inevitable detection. If you're curious, look up TrickBot, Emotet, or QakBot. They kind of defined what it means to be modular. They also kind of gave birth to what's known as "MaaS" (Malware-as-a-Service).  State-sponsored Threat Actors malware is trickier because it needs to be designed for a target. For example, when the United States (allegedly) targeted the Chinese government (allegedly) as APT NightEagle (allegedly) the malware was developed to work almost exclusively for specific Chinese infrastructure and (allegedly) contained exploits which would work in ideal scenarios which (allegedly) were that of Chinese critical infrastructure.  This can also be seen with what the Russian government alleges the United States and Israel (allegedly) did with Operation Triangulation whereas the malware (allegedly) only worked for specific sets of hardware (allegedly). Furthermore, this can also (allegedly) be seen with the United States (allegedly) purchasing cell phone malware from Israeli companies (allegedly) which were developed and sold to ICE (allegedly) to spy on people critical of ICE (allegedly).  These companies are called NSO Group and Intellexa Alliance.  Of course, the United States and Israel government vehemently deny the allegations from the Chinese and Russian government.  Okay, I have to stop writing and schizo ranting for the time being. I have to go back to watching a baby and stuff.

I was a disaster of a human being when I was constantly searching for people who would "meet my needs." I'd obsess about all the ways my lovers and friends weren't "meeting my needs," which were really just these specific acts and services I'd elevated in my mind as *needs* because I wanted an external reason I could obsess about as to why I wasn't happy. At some point I realized the point of a relationship was not to have my needs fulfilled, it was to express love and help the light of goodness move through me and into other people. Only when I let go of the idea of needed to be satisfied and fulfilled by my relationships, was I actually satisfied.  And ultimately, I discovered the truth is that there was one person who could fulfill it all my needs. It was me.

🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-06-16 21:58 KST
🟠 위협 점수: 8/10 (높음)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 공격자들은 **Fortinet FortiSandbox**와 같은 보안 장비의 취약점을 직접 타격하여 방어 체계를 무력화하고, **Microsoft Teams** 인프라를 C2 통신 채널로 악용하는 등 은밀한 침투 기술을 고도화하고 있습니다. 또한, AI 모델(Vertex AI, LangGraph)의 설정 오류나 공급망 취약점을 이용한 RCE 공격이 증가하고 있으며, 대규모 데이터 유출을 동반한 이중 협박(Double Extortion)이 제약 및 교육 분야를 중심으로 지속되고 있습니다. 특히, 보안 담당자의 심리적 허점을 노린 스피어 피싱과 정상 IT 도구를 악용하는 'Living-off-the-land' 기법이 위협의 주류를 이루고 있습니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
- **UNC6508 (Mandiant/CyberScoop):** 중국 연계 그룹으로, 미국 의료·연구 기관을 타겟으로 1년 이상 잠복하며 AI 및 국방 관련 민감 데이터를 탈취.
- **ShinyHunters / UNC6240 (Mandiant):** Oracle PeopleSoft 취약점을 악용하여 교육 분야를 대상으로 데이터 유출 및 금전적 갈등 유도.
- **DragonForce (BleepingComputer):** Microsoft Teams 릴레이 인프라를 C2 통신에 악용하는 커스텀 악성코드 'Backdoor.Turn' 사용.
- **Kimsuky (이스트시큐리티 ESRC):** '개인정보 유출'을 사칭한 스피어 피싱 메일을 통해 국내 보안 담당자 및 기관을 타겟팅.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
- **Kimsuky:** 국내 보안 담당자를 대상으로 개인정보 유출을 사칭한 스피어 피싱 공격 수행.
- **npm 패키지 악용:** Solana SDK를 사칭한 악성 npm 패키지 9종이 발견되어 Telegram Bot API 기반의 RAT 유포 확인.
- **개인정보위 권고:** 공공기관 및 클라우드 환경 내 API 키, 접근 토큰 등 자격증명 관리 부실로 인한 유출 사고 증가에 따른 보안 강화 권고.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점 (CVSS 우선)
- **CVE-2026-39813, CVE-2026-39808, CVE-2026-25089:** Fortinet FortiSandbox, CVSS 9.1, **Active Exploitation**.
- **CVE-2026-53633:** Vitest (Browser Mode), CVSS 9.8, RCE 취약점.
- **CVE-2026-54420:** LiteSpeed cPanel plugin, CVSS 8.5, **Active Exploitation** (CISA KEV 등재).
- **CVE-2026-53435:** Jenkins, CVSS 8.8, **Active Exploitation**.
- **CVE-2026-20262:** Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, CVSS 미상(High), **Active Exploitation**.

### 5. IOC 요약
- **해시:**
    - `a0104921a2d37ab87482ac9a9f5c3713` ([SANS ISC](https://isc.sans.edu/diary/rss/33080))
- **CVE:**
    - `CVE-2026-39813, CVE-2026-39808, CVE-2026-25089` ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/attackers-exploit-three-fortinet.html))
    - `CVE-2026-54420` ([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/cisa-warns-of-another-actively-exploited-cpanel-plugin-flaw/))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
- **Fortinet/cPanel 패치:** FortiSandbox 및 LiteSpeed cPanel 플러그인 취약점(CVE-2026-39813 등)에 대한 즉각적인 보안 패치 적용.
- **자격증명 감사:** 클라우드 환경 및 개발 협업 도구(GitHub, npm 등) 내 하드코딩된 API 키, 인증 토큰 노출 여부 전수 조사 및 로테이션 수행.
- **C2 트래픽 모니터링:** Microsoft Teams 릴레이 등 정상적인 SaaS 인프라를 경유하는 비정상적인 아웃바운드 트래픽 패턴 탐지 룰셋 강화.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
